skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem:
y = 6p(q + 11)

The given equation relates the positive numbers p, q, and y. Which equation correctly expresses p in terms of q and y?
rationale: Choice B is correct. To express p in terms of q and y, divide both sides of the given equation by 6(q + 11). Since q is positive, q + 11 ≠ 0, so the division is valid, giving p = y/(6(q + 11)). Choice A is equivalent to y = p/(6(q+11)). Choice C is equivalent to y = 6(p + q + 11). Choice D is equivalent to y = 6(q+11)/p.
